idea: Weekly surge in Google Trends for 'home workout equipment' signals discretionary recovery

data.source: Google Trends weekly search interest for 'home workout equipment'

behavior: Rising interest in home fitness gear often precedes increased discretionary spending on related consumer products.

market_impact.rationale: Increased fitness interest drives discretionary retail sales growth.

trade_logic.entry: Entry when 2-week moving average of search interest rises by more than 15% week-over-week
trade_logic.exit: Exit when search interest falls below 5% week-over-week or after 4 weeks

reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Google Trends data for common consumer keywords regularly shows weekly spikes.

idea: Weekly spike in labor_jobs job postings for warehouse roles predicts industrial strength

data.source: Labor job postings for warehouse roles from labor_jobs adapter

behavior: Increasing warehouse job postings reflect rising demand for logistics capacity supporting industrial activity.

market_impact.rationale: Industrial sector benefits from expanding warehouse and logistics employment.

trade_logic.entry: Entry when warehouse job postings increase by more than 10% over prior 2 weeks
trade_logic.exit: Exit when postings decline 5% or after 6 weeks

reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Warehouse job postings fluctuate weekly with logistics demand cycles.

idea: Daily spike in consumer stress Google search for 'budgeting apps' signals impending retail caution

data.source: Google Trends daily search interest for 'budgeting apps'

behavior: Rising interest in budgeting apps indicates growing consumer financial stress and potential spending pullback.

market_impact.rationale: Consumer stress reduces discretionary spending, pressuring retail.

trade_logic.entry: Entry when daily search interest spikes by more than 20% over prior 3 days
trade_logic.exit: Exit after 10 trading days or once search interest normalizes below 5% spike

reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Daily Google Trends data for finance-related queries frequently spike.
